summary: Matched several small fighter and library functions by replacing nonmatching stubs with simple C implementations, adding required includes, and correcting a ftCommonData field type from UNK_T to float. The PR summary claimed five 100% matches, including fn_8002087C, lbRefract_800225D4, ftCo_Rebirth_Coll, ftCo_CatchDash_Phys, and ftAnim_80070F28, but the provided diff excerpt only shows four new implementations and the type/include fixes. Review feedback specifically endorsed changing ftCommonData::x64 to float instead of using a cast at the callsite.
what_changed: Implemented ftCo_CatchDash_Phys in ftCo_Attack100.c. The function gets Fighter from gobj->user_data and calls ft_80085030 with p_ftCommonData->x64 multiplied by fp->co_attrs.gr_friction and fp->facing_dir.
Implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Coll in ft_0D31.c. The function gets Fighter via GET_FIGHTER(gobj), checks fp->smash_attrs.x2135 == -1, and calls ft_80083DCC(gobj) when true.
Added include ft/ft_081B.h in ft_0D31.c, apparently needed for the newly used collision/helper symbol.

Implemented ftAnim_80070F28 in ftanim.c. It iterates over fp->x8B0 with ARRAY_SIZE, checks active slots by casting slot->x11 to s8 and comparing against -1, clears flags_b5 for all part indices listed by fp->ft_data->x1C[i], then resets slot->x11 to -1.
Changed struct ftCommonData field at offset +0x64 from UNK_T x64 to float x64 in ft/types.h.
Implemented lbRefract_800225D4 in lbrefract.c. It calls GXSetTevDirect(0), GXSetNumIndStages(0), and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
Added include baselib/state.h in lbrefract.c for HSD_StateInvalidate.

The PR body also says fn_8002087C in lbbgflash.c was matched and configure.py was fixed to keep partially matched files as NonMatching, but those file changes are not present in the provided changed-files list or diff excerpt, so evidence for those items is weak in this slice.
smart_moves: Promoted ftCommonData::x64 to float rather than preserving an unsafe local cast at each use; this both matches the observed arithmetic and improves future callsites.
Used existing fighter-access idioms consistently: direct gobj->user_data where surrounding code likely did so, and GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in ft_0D31.c.
Kept ftAnim_80070F28 structured around existing data layout: fp->x8B0 slots, ft_data->x1C per-slot metadata, part index lists, and parts[part_idx].flags_b5.
Used ARRAY_SIZE for the x8B0 loop, avoiding hardcoded slot counts.
Matched lbRefract_800225D4 as a minimal render-state reset wrapper using existing GX and HSD state APIs, with the correct header include added for CI.
decomp_lessons: If a value is consistently used as a floating-point coefficient, prefer fixing the struct field type to float over inserting pointer casts or bit reinterpretation in matched code.
Small matches often require header hygiene: adding the exact include for newly referenced helpers or baselib APIs can be necessary for CI even when local context makes the symbol obvious.
For data-driven fighter animation cleanup, preserve signed sentinel behavior explicitly; this PR used `(s8)slot->x11 != -1` and then assigned `slot->x11 = -1`.
When matching code that clears per-part flags, model the original indirection through ftData_x1C and its parts list instead of simplifying to a broader loop over all parts.
Library render-state functions may match as short sequences of external API calls; includes such as baselib/state.h matter when introducing HSD_StateInvalidate.
naming_conventions: Matched unknown functions retained address-style names such as ftAnim_80070F28 and lbRefract_800225D4.
Common fighter functions use the ftCo_ prefix, for example ftCo_CatchDash_Phys and ftCo_Rebirth_Coll.
Fighter struct placeholder fields retain offset-style names, for example x8B0, x11, x2135, and ftCommonData::x64.
The corrected ftCommonData field kept its offset name x64 while improving its type to float, rather than inventing a semantic name without enough evidence.
Local variables in the excerpt follow existing decomp conventions such as fp for Fighter*, gobj for HSD_GObj/Fighter_GObj, slot for indexed struct entries, and part_idx for part indices.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use struct field type corrections to eliminate casts and produce the desired float arithmetic codegen.
For sentinel byte fields, cast to s8 before comparing with -1 when the underlying field type may not already be signed.
Use ARRAY_SIZE on fixed arrays to encourage clean loop bounds and reduce magic constants.
Leave unknown or weakly understood field names as offset-based placeholders while matching behavior.
Introduce only the minimal control flow required by the observed behavior, such as the single conditional in ftCo_Rebirth_Coll and the nested loops in ftAnim_80070F28.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: You can just change the type of `x64` to `float`.
review_feedback.lesson: Prefer correcting the shared struct declaration when the type is known, instead of using `*(float*)&p_ftCommonData->x64` at a use site.

follow_up_queries: Check the full PR or repository history for the claimed fn_8002087C/lbbgflash.c and configure.py changes, because they are mentioned in the PR body but absent from the provided diff excerpt.
Search for remaining uses of ftCommonData::x64 to see whether the float type unlocks more matches or removes other casts.
Compare ftAnim_80070F28 with neighboring ftAnim functions to refine names for x8B0, x11, ftData_x1C, and flags_b5 once more behavior is understood.
Look for other render-state reset functions around lbRefract_800225D4 and lbRefract_80022BB8 to infer better semantic names.
